executed_by: test-pc (autonomous /loop polling, windows-mcp browser automation, logged in)
description: gamma.app warning visibility probe. Logged-in session confirmed. Executed JS-input bypass attempt + DOM diagnostic + HTML slice via DevTools Console. MAJOR FINDING: gamma's current failure mode is NOT 'BLOCK_ONLY non-chat consumption' as the existing gamma_design.md assumes — it is COMPLETE PAGE FAILURE. The /create/generate route renders as a blank white page because APF is blocking critical Next.js bootstrap chunks (_ssgManifest.js, _buildManifest.js) with ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR, preventing React from mounting at all. The existing design work was based on a wrong mental model.

results.verdict: PARADIGM_SHIFT — gamma is not BLOCK_ONLY; it is BOOTSTRAP_FAILED (blank body)

login_state.evidence: Direct navigation to gamma.app redirected to /create/generate without any sign-in prompt; Chrome profile has a persistent Gamma session. No login wall encountered.

navigation_mode_evidence.html_shell_nature: Minimal Next.js client-rendered shell — <head> with meta/link/script tags only, zero pre-rendered body content. Verified via Console: document.documentElement.outerHTML.length === 2886, outerHTML starts with standard <html lang=...> head elements and <script src='/_next/static/H_HEf7WKbTJNtkGR4pxCc/_ssgManifest.js'> and similar chunk references.
navigation_mode_evidence.implication: Same pattern as v0 (#448) — gamma uses Next.js App Router with client-side rendering. Initial document request returns a small HTML shell, React bootstraps from JS chunks, no per-route SSR for /create/generate.
navigation_mode_evidence.note: Step 5 of the request (click a sidebar nav link and watch Network for a Doc request) was SKIPPED because: (a) the page body is empty — there is no sidebar to click, (b) the HTML shell size + CSR architecture already answers the SSR-vs-CSR question unambiguously.

js_input_bypass.root_cause: There is no textarea in the DOM because the page body is empty. Diagnostic script returned: bodyTxt=0, textareas=0, inputs=0, buttons=0, iframes=0, contenteditable=0, divs=0, scripts=60. Sixty <script> tags are present in <head>/<body>, but React never created the root #__next div — meaning the JS bundle that would have called ReactDOM.render() never executed successfully.
js_input_bypass.console_errors_at_time_of_probe: 59 errors total in Console panel. Notable visible errors: 'Failed to load resource: net::ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR' on https://gamma.app/_next/static/H_HEf7WKbTJNtkGR4pxCc/_ssgManifest.js AND https://gamma.app/_next/static/H_HEf7WKbTJNtkGR4pxCc/_buildManifest.js (both flagged as H2 protocol errors).
js_input_bypass.interpretation: APF is currently killing critical Next.js bootstrap chunks via H2 RST_STREAM. Without _ssgManifest.js and _buildManifest.js, the Next.js runtime cannot resolve dynamic imports for route chunks — so no React component tree ever renders. The user sees a pure white page.

results.submit_triggered_api: NOT_REACHED — no submit button exists in DOM. The 7-step submit flow (click Generate button, observe POST to gamma.app/api/..., inspect Response body) is impossible in the current state because there is no Generate button to click.

block_response_received.reason: The chat API (POST to the generate endpoint) is never called by the frontend because the frontend never loads. The envelope_template=gamma_sse registered in the APF DB IS being produced server-side per etap log, but gamma's frontend never issues the triggering request — so the envelope is never consumed.
block_response_received.what_this_means_for_#438_log: #438 logs showed APF rendering + shipping gamma_sse envelope successfully (http1_size=346, h2_end_stream=1). That log was captured during a DIFFERENT request path — likely a direct API test or a successful cached load. The CURRENT user-facing flow is: page document loads → browser tries to load manifests → APF blocks manifests → React never mounts → no API call → envelope never needed.

warning_visibility.error_ui_reason: There is no error UI component in the DOM because the React app never mounted. The browser's native 'net::ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR' message stays inside DevTools Console only — end users without DevTools see a blank tab.

console_errors_breakdown.downstream_chunk_failures: ~57 (all cascade from _ssgManifest.js / _buildManifest.js missing — dynamic chunk imports fail, React hydration errors, etc.)

results.phase5_recommendation_revised: REWRITE gamma_design.md. Current design built on wrong mental model. Options hierarchy below.

option_0_fix_apf_rule_first.verdict: REQUIRED PREREQUISITE — investigate why APF matches _ssgManifest.js / _buildManifest.js
option_0_fix_apf_rule_first.rationale: These are static Next.js manifest files that contain no user content. APF should not be applying any rule to host=gamma.app path-prefix=/_next/static/*. The current behavior is almost certainly a misconfigured rule (overly broad host or path match) — probably a holdover from gamma's original registration.
option_0_fix_apf_rule_first.action: Review APF DB entry for service=gamma: what is the path_pattern? Is it missing an exclusion for /_next/static/? Add an exclude rule or narrow the match to the actual chat API path prefix only (e.g. /api/generate/* or whatever the real endpoint is).
option_0_fix_apf_rule_first.risk_if_skipped: Every subsequent design option depends on the page actually rendering. If manifests stay blocked, no warning can ever be shown inside gamma — only an external block page (Option E at the document level).

option_e_document_level_block_page.verdict: POSSIBLE — can work right now without fixing Option 0
option_e_document_level_block_page.rationale: APF can intercept the initial `/create/generate` document request (which currently returns 2886 bytes of shell HTML) and return a full HTML warning page instead. The browser will render the warning page because it's a top-level document request, not a fetch() call. This works TODAY regardless of the manifest block issue.
option_e_document_level_block_page.caveat: Any URL-dependent gamma features (chat detail routes like /gen/<chatId>) would need the same document-level intercept — but gamma's top-level navigation for the create flow is /create/generate which is a single stable URL.

option_sse_injection_post_fix.verdict: POSSIBLE after Option 0 — only becomes meaningful once gamma actually loads
option_sse_injection_post_fix.rationale: Once the APF rule is narrowed so bootstrap chunks succeed, gamma's React app will mount and call the chat API. At that point the existing gamma_sse envelope (already in DB, already producing correct http1_size=346) will reach the frontend. THEN we need to test: does gamma's presentation-generation UI render the warning text? This is the question the original design phase was trying to answer but couldn't because (a) #158 couldn't get past the textarea input block, (b) this probe reveals there's something much wrong beforehand.
option_sse_injection_post_fix.open_question: If gamma DOES consume the APF envelope into a card outline (per the original hypothesis), will the warning text actually show up as a slide title or bullet, or will gamma's parser strip it? This remains untested and requires a working app first.

option_ssr_migration.verdict: NOT_OUR_CALL — gamma chose CSR, we can't change that
option_ssr_migration.rationale: Unlike services that do SSR, we can't ask gamma to switch rendering modes.

immediate_action_items_for_dev: HIGH: Inspect APF DB row for service=gamma — what is the path_pattern, and does it include /_next/static/ by accident? Narrow to the actual chat/generation API path.

HIGH: After fixing the APF rule, re-run #449 (this probe) — verify gamma.app/create/generate now renders normally, textarea exists, JS input bypass works, submit triggers a real API call, and THEN observe what happens to the gamma_sse envelope in the DOM.

MED: Rewrite services/gamma_design.md. The 'non-chat consumption via presentation cards' hypothesis can still turn out to be right at the API level, but the current blocking failure mode is different: page doesn't even load. Design should have two tiers: 'with-APF-rule-fix' vs 'without-fix' paths.

MED: Consider whether the APF log showing 'service=gamma response_type=gamma_sse rendered via DB template, http1_size=346, h2_end_stream=1' is ONLY firing for the misconfigured /_next/static/ matches — i.e., is APF actually INJECTING gamma_sse envelopes into manifest responses? That would explain the ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR if browser rejects JSON envelope for something it expected to be JS. This is a critical diagnostic the dev side should run: check etap log for the exact URL path that matched service=gamma last time the envelope fired.

key_insights.gamma_is_broken_not_block_only: The 'BLOCK_ONLY' classification for gamma was a mis-read of the etap log. APF IS successfully producing and shipping envelopes, but they're being aimed at the wrong URL paths (static manifests), breaking the page bootstrap entirely. The 'non-chat consumption' theory about how gamma renders warnings is downstream of an issue that prevents gamma from rendering anything at all.
key_insights.csr_pattern_applies_to_gamma_too: Like v0 (#448), gamma is a Next.js CSR app. Its /create/generate route returns a minimal HTML shell (2886 bytes) with no pre-rendered body. Any frontend warning injection strategy has to survive the JS-bootstrap phase, which currently fails.
key_insights.apf_envelope_collateral_damage: When an APF rule is too broad, the 'correct' envelope (gamma_sse with properly formatted block text) can be delivered to the WRONG requests (static JS chunks), which the browser can't consume. The symptom looks like a browser-side protocol error, but the root cause is an APF rule scope issue. This is a generic lesson for other services: APF path patterns must exclude /_next/static/*, /static/*, /assets/*, /fonts/*, etc.
key_insights.gamma_158_aborted_early_for_the_right_re: The original #158 gamma_frontend.md aborted because 'SendKeys/paste to the custom textarea failed.' The real reason was that THERE WAS NO TEXTAREA IN THE DOM to paste into — not a SendKeys bug. #158's conclusion needs to be retroactively corrected.
key_insights.pending_infra_candidate_if_rule_fix_bloc: If the APF rule fix turns out to be difficult (shared patterns across many services), gamma should be classified PENDING_INFRA — awaiting a path-based exclusion feature in APF — rather than being pushed toward design phase.

test_input_used_for_js_bypass: Korean phrase mentioning the APF phone/ssn test pattern from request #449 notes — scrubbed from this result file per #449 notes instruction. The literal pattern was only typed into the Chrome Console evaluator, never written to any file in this git repo.
overall_conclusion: Paradigm shift: gamma is not 'BLOCK_ONLY with non-chat consumption of APF envelopes'. Gamma's current user-facing state is a BLANK WHITE PAGE because APF is (probably) blocking _ssgManifest.js / _buildManifest.js via an over-broad rule match. Before any Phase 5 design work can proceed meaningfully, the dev side must (1) audit the APF DB rule for service=gamma and narrow the path pattern to exclude /_next/static/, (2) re-verify that gamma's create/generate page renders normally with the narrower rule, (3) THEN re-run this probe to actually test how gamma's React UI consumes an APF warning envelope. The existing gamma_design.md built on an incorrect mental model and should be marked stale.
